title: Rubus coriifrons
intro: Rubus coriifolius, also known as Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan bramble, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant is rich in health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as digestive issues and skin problems. The bioactive constituents of Rubus coriifolius include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional medicines and skincare products.
taxonomy: Rubus coriifrons is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a member of the Rubus genus, which includes blackberries, raspberries, and thimbleberries. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Asia. As a member of the Rubus genus, Rubus coriifrons is closely related to other species of bramble plants. The exact classification of Rubus coriifrons is still being studied and refined by botanists.
